items.summary: President Trump and Iran’s lead negotiator have signed an agreement that was expected to open the Strait of Hormuz. But the specific terms of the deal have not been released, and Israel’s leader appears not to be fully on board.

items.title: Morgan Stanley Cuts Oil Forecasts as Hormuz Deal Revives Supply
items.summary: Morgan Stanley made substantial cuts to its oil-price forecasts for the coming quarters as the interim deal between the US and Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz stands to revive regional output and boost supplies.

items.title: Woodside Energy’s Louisiana LNG President Abruptly Departs
items.summary: The top executive at Woodside Energy Group Ltd.’s $17.5 billion liquefied natural gas project in Louisiana has departed, effective Monday, just over a year after she was hired, according to a company memo seen by Bloomberg.

items.title: Iranian American protesters gather as football team prepare for World Cup opener
items.summary:
Iranian Americans streamed into the Los Angeles stadium where Iran play their first match at the 2026 World Cup on Monday, with some calling for Iranians to band together and forget politics while others bore symbols protesting the government.

After US-Iran deal, Netanyahu says he will run in coming Israeli elections
items.summary:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said he intends to run in elections scheduled for later this year, as he faces domestic criticism over his wartime leadership.
Netanyahu, 76, is Israel’s longest-serving prime minister, has led his country through three years of war on multiple interconnected fronts and is currently on trial for corruption.
In recent months, he has come under mou

items.title: Zelensky proposes meeting Putin in US after Russian leader ‘ignores’ G7 invitation
items.summary:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Monday he was willing to meet Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin in the United States, suggesting Putin would find such an offer “harder to refuse”.
The Ukrainian leader said he made the proposal in a call with US President Donald Trump, and was waiting to see what came of it.
Putin has repeatedly rejected offers for a face-to-face meeting with Zelens

items.title: Can India react to Gulf sailors’ deaths like China did with US after 1999 embassy bombing?
items.summary:
The images were difficult to ignore. A viral video showed the body of a 35-year-old Indian seafarer on a vessel off Oman, with crew members using cold water bottles in a desperate attempt to slow the decomposition process.
He had died last Thursday from medical complications, but it had not been possible to send help or evacuate him from the ship because of the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.
